question: Can the existing ALife generators produce a schema-clean, deterministic, byte-replayable gameplay-event corpus across native planes and bounded high-dimensional lattice worlds?

hypotheses.prediction: Every sampled episode reproduces its canonical event JSONL byte for byte from its frozen config, seed, and code hash.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any sampled episode differs at any event byte, fails schema validation, or lacks an exact replay receipt.

model.update_order: Native episodes use the existing synchronous four-plane LifeUniverse update. Lattice episodes use synchronous totalistic updates on periodic dimensions 4-11.
model.rng: Native episodes use the existing random.seed(seed) stream without added draws. Lattice episodes use numpy.default_rng(seed) for initialization and deterministic updates thereafter. Episodes execute sequentially.

seed_plan.production: Seeds 10000 through 10499, one independently initialized episode per seed.
seed_plan.pairing: No causal pairing claim; numeric seeds identify complete episode RNG streams.

exposure.eligibility: Every planned condition initializes its declared native or lattice world.
exposure.activation: Every initialized world executes at least one synchronous update.
exposure.state_change: Per-tick births, deaths, and applicable mechanic events are retained as canonical JSONL.

analysis.uncertainty: None claimed: this is a bounded generator corpus and deterministic replay audit, not inferential evidence.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: Not applicable. Coverage and complexity are diagnostics and never acceptance gates.
analysis.stopping_rule: Stop at 500 completed episodes, 7200 wall seconds, 4096 MB process RSS, 3072 MB retained artifacts, failed schema validation, or failed deterministic replay.
analysis.acceptance_boundary: Chronicle output is data, not ontology or coverage evidence. No registry changes are authorized.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution; no worker parallelism.
